completion_summary: Froze and audited a post-confirmatory SafeGP failure diagnostic on new Dev seeds 1300-1304 with 40 complete experiments per run. Compared random and structured SafeGP beta 2.0/1.5/1.0. Beta 2.0 retained the best mean flow conversion (0.05756), lowest risk exceedance (0.02), and lowest cost per experiment (0.39776); lower-beta variants had negative paired objective effects versus the incumbent and cost regression above 5%, so no revision was selected. All 20 runs replay-verified with complete resource ledgers. The report exposes decision wall time, process CPU, and total wall time and explicitly cannot repair the prior confirmatory miss. Six diagnostic/architecture tests plus Ruff/mypy pass.
notes: Use only new Dev seeds 1300-1304. Preserve the failed confirmatory conclusion and reject any beta change that does not jointly improve objective, risk, and cost against the incumbent.
scope: Freeze and audit the fresh-seed SafeGP beta failure diagnostic, retain the incumbent when lower confidence beta does not produce a joint objective-risk-cost improvement, and expose classical decision compute costs
